meta.title: St. George's Square (Misraħ San Ġorġ)
meta.description: Complete travel guide to St. George's Square in Valletta, Malta. Learn about this historic square, including location, history, and visitor information.

intro.title: Discover St. George's Square
intro.description: St. George's Square, also known as Misraħ San Ġorġ, is one of the most famous squares in Valletta, the capital city of Malta. Located in the heart of the city, it is surrounded by important historic buildings.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Visit the square early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the heat.
Combine your visit with nearby historic attractions like St. John's Co-Cathedral.
Enjoy the Baroque architecture of the buildings surrounding the square.

sections.title: Historical Background
sections.content: St. George's Square was constructed at the same time as Valletta in 1566. The square was part of the original city plan and has great historical importance in Malta's life.

sections.title: Architecture & Layout
sections.content: The square is surrounded by splendid Baroque buildings, including St. John's Co-Cathedral and historic administrative buildings. The design reflects the capital city's architecture.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: St. George's Square is central to cultural events in Malta. It is often used for concerts, public events, and national celebrations.

sections.title: Preservation
sections.content: The square and surrounding buildings are protected under Malta's cultural heritage laws. The Heritage Malta agency takes care of their maintenance.

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.parkTime: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
hours.bestTimeSpring: Spring: Pleasant weather, ideal for walking
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Cooler in mornings and evenings
hours.bestTimeAutumn: Autumn: Mild weather, perfect for leisurely walks
hours.bestTimeWinter: Winter: Fewer tourists, quiet appreciation of the square
hours.tip: The square is open 24 hours, but it's recommended to visit during daylight for the best experience.

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: The square is located in the heart of Valletta, easily accessible by walking.
transport.fromStationDesc: About 10 minutes walk from Valletta Bus Terminal.
transport.fromAirportDesc: About 30 minutes by car from Malta Airport, use X4 bus.
transport.publicTransportDesc: The square is served by many bus lines stopping nearby.
transport.walkingDesc: The best way to explore Valletta is on foot, as the city is small and compact.
transport.drivingDesc: Given the narrow streets in Valletta, it is recommended to park in public parking lots in the city and walk.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Experience the history and culture of Valletta on this route.
steps: Start at St. George's Square and admire its Baroque architecture
Walk towards the Grand Master's Palace
End with a view of the Grand Harbour from Upper Barrakka Gardens

spots.description: Visit the square at night when it's lit by ambient lighting.
